task_completion.description: Create a 'Monthly_Variance' sheet calculating monthly budget, actual, dollar variance, and percentage variance.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A worksheet tab named 'Monthly_Variance' is visible. The sheet contains columns for Budget, Actual, Dollar Variance (or $ Var), and Percentage Variance (or % Var) across the cost centers and categories.

task_completion.description: Create a 'YTD_Summary' sheet showing year-to-date totals and a trend indicator.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A worksheet tab named 'YTD_Summary' is visible. The sheet displays aggregated YTD totals for budget, actuals, and variances, along with a specific column for trend indicators comparing to the prior quarter.

task_completion.description: Create an 'Executive_Summary' sheet identifying total YTD variance, top 3 unfavorable cost centers, top 3 favorable categories, and % of items exceeding budget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A worksheet tab named 'Executive_Summary' is visible. The sheet clearly lists four key metrics: overall company YTD variance, a top 3 list of unfavorable cost centers, a top 3 list of favorable expense categories, and a percentage representing line items over budget.

task_completion.description: Apply conditional formatting for variances > 5% (red/green) and use icon sets for the YTD trend.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Cells containing variance percentages greater than 5% are highlighted in red (unfavorable) or green (favorable). The YTD trend column contains visual icon sets (e.g., directional arrows, traffic lights, or shapes).

task_completion.description: Generate a clustered bar chart on the YTD_Summary sheet comparing YTD Budget vs Actual by cost center.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A clustered bar chart is present on the 'YTD_Summary' sheet. The chart's horizontal or vertical axis lists the 8 cost centers, and there are two distinct bars (Budget and Actual) clustered together for each cost center.

task_completion.description: Generate a line chart on the Executive_Summary sheet showing the 12-month total variance trend.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line chart is present on the 'Executive_Summary' sheet. The chart displays a single continuous line plotting the total variance across 12 data points (months).
The spreadsheet application's title bar shows the filename 'budget_analysis.xlsx', or a file explorer window shows this file successfully saved in the Documents directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the spreadsheet application's graphical user interface to build the dashboard.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show an actual spreadsheet application (like Excel or LibreOffice Calc) open with active menus, ribbons, or dialog boxes being used, rather than just a terminal running a Python script.

integrity.description: The agent used formulas to calculate the summaries rather than hardcoding the answers.
integrity.visual_evidence: When a summary cell is selected in the screenshots, the formula bar shows spreadsheet functions (e.g., LARGE, SMALL, INDEX, MATCH, SUM) rather than static typed numbers.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply copy and paste the task instructions into the spreadsheet.
integrity.visual_evidence: The spreadsheet contains structured financial data, formatted tables, and charts, not raw paragraphs of text from the prompt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The workbook must contain exactly 3 new sheets: 'Monthly_Variance', 'YTD_Summary', and 'Executive_Summary'. The source data has exactly 8 cost centers, 28 expense categories, and 12 months. The Executive Summary must use advanced formulas (like LARGE/SMALL with INDEX-MATCH) to find the top 3 unfavorable cost centers and top 3 favorable categories. Conditional formatting must include red/green colors for >5% variance and icon sets for the YTD trend. Charts required: a clustered bar chart on YTD_Summary and a line trend chart on Executive_Summary.
